text.title: Golden-Master Corpus for AI-Driven Legacy Rewrites Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in real deployments of this methodology. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Tests use `assert similar(a, b, threshold=0.9)`. AI rewrite passes with 91% match; production bugs follow.
antipattern.root-cause: Author allowed fuzzy comparison to avoid canonicalizing non-determinism.
antipattern.fix: Replace soft equality with byte-for-byte after canonicalization; canonicalize known non-determinism explicitly.

antipattern.symptom: 200 diffs in the corpus; reviewer approves them all in one click. Three were regressions.
antipattern.root-cause: Approval UI lets you approve the entire diff set.
antipattern.fix: Force row-by-row approval; reject any approval commit that updates more than N rows without per-row sign-off.

antipattern.symptom: Inputs sampled with `random.choice(traffic)`. Tail cases (negative quantities, unicode names) never sampled; bug ships.
antipattern.fix: Use stratified sampling: top-N most frequent + N tail samples + N adversarial fuzz inputs.
